mechanism_narrative: A higher FEDFUNDS setting relative to recent months tightens financial conditions and lifts Treasury term-premium expectations over the next 20 trading days, pushing the 10-year yield upward as markets price less easing and more persistent policy restraint.
failure_condition: If 10-year yield changes are negative in most 20-day windows when FEDFUNDS is flat or rising, or if the sign relationship flips for more than two consecutive years in walk-forward testing, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates after a prior monthly lull, inflation persistence rises and bond investors demand higher nominal compensation, so the 10-year yield rises over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above its prior 6-month median
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances produce flat or falling 10-year yields more than 60% of the time, or if the average 20-day yield change is not positive after inflation acceleration events.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and decline in UNRATE signals labor-market strength that increases growth and inflation expectations, which lifts the 10-year yield within the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either proxy alone in predicting 20-day yield rises, or if positive yield changes do not dominate when PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls together.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in RSAFS indicates weaker consumer demand and softer nominal activity, reducing expected real growth and lowering the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls for two consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS declines are not followed by lower 10-year yields more often than not, or if the average 20-day yield response after two-month retail-sales declines is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A broad decline in INDPRO signals weaker industrial capacity utilization and lower cyclical inflation pressure, which pulls the 10-year yield down over the next 5 trading days as rate markets reprice a softer output backdrop.
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon yield changes are not negative following falling INDPRO readings, or if the sign relationship disappears in rolling one-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST and GDPC1 both improve, housing demand and aggregate demand strengthen together, leading markets to price a firmer growth path and higher long rates over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint housing-and-GDP improvements do not produce positive 60-day yield changes more often than either series alone, or if the effect is absent in expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary labor market, a rise in UNRATE above a modest threshold signals slack and weaker wage pressure, so the 10-year yield falls over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points from its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-threshold breaches are followed by positive or flat 10-year yield changes in most cases, or if the conditional hit rate is not better than unconditional yield-direction frequency.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL remains elevated while FEDFUNDS is not rising, real-policy restraint erodes and bond markets anticipate future tightening; this combination pushes the 10-year yield upward over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL stays above its 6-month trend while FEDFUNDS is unchanged or lower for two consecutive monthly prints
failure_condition: Reject if elevated inflation without higher policy rates does not precede rising 10-year yields over 60 trading days, or if the interaction term is not significant in rolling out-of-sample tests.
